resources.string: Android is a mobile operating system based on a modified Linux kernel and other open-source software, designed primarily for touchscreen mobile devices such as smartphones and tablets.
iOS is a mobile operating system created and developed by Apple Inc. exclusively for its hardware. It is the second most popular mobile OS in the world after Android.
HarmonyOS is a distributed operating system developed by Huawei. It is designed to support various types of devices, including smartphones, tablets, PCs, and IoT devices.
Windows Mobile is a family of mobile operating systems developed by Microsoft for smartphones and PDA devices.
KaiOS is a Linux-based operating system designed for feature phones with physical keyboards. It supports 4G, Wi-Fi, GPS, and modern web apps.
BlackBerry OS is a mobile operating system developed by BlackBerry Limited for the line of BlackBerry smartphones.
Sailfish OS is a GNU/Linux-based mobile operating system developed by Jolla. It is designed for smartphones and tablets.
Tizen is an open source, standardized software platform for various device categories, including smartphones, tablets, and smart TVs.
